explanation: The 3-model ensemble (t_learner_ridge + r_learner + hurdle) with ci_weights achieves the highest holdout lower CI (0.0971) across all 108 combinations tested. This represents a 2.9% improvement over the best single model (hurdle at 0.0944) and 1.1% improvement over the best pair. The CI-based weighting appropriately emphasizes t_learner_ridge (0.39) and hurdle (0.346) which had the strongest CV performance, while still incorporating r_learner (0.264) for diversity. These three models represent genuinely different approaches: t_learner_ridge (linear method), r_learner (residual-based), and hurdle (two-stage). This methodological diversity reduces overfitting risk. The fact that equal weighting (rank 4, 0.0954) performs nearly as well validates that this isn't a lucky weighting but rather reflects true complementarity among the models.
alternatives_considered: Single hurdle model (0.0944): Strong baseline but leaves 2.9% performance on table
t_learner_ridge + hurdle pair with ci_weights (0.0960): Simpler 2-model option, but 3rd model (r_learner) adds meaningful lift (+1.1%)
Same 3-model combo with equal weights (0.0954): Slightly worse than ci_weights, confirming CV-informed weighting adds value
dr_learner combinations: Generally underperform despite being in CV results, showing holdout reveals its limitations
overfitting_assessment: Low overfitting risk. Key evidence: (1) The selected ensemble's CV performance aligns with holdout results - all three models had strong CV metrics (t_learner_ridge: 0.0964, hurdle: 0.0898, r_learner: 0.0705). (2) Multiple weighting strategies for this same model combination rank in top 4 (ci_weights #1, equal weights #4), indicating robustness. (3) The 2.9% improvement over single model is substantial but not extreme, suggesting real signal rather than noise fitting. (4) The models employ fundamentally different causal estimation approaches (linear separability, residualization, two-stage), making them unlikely to overfit in the same way. (5) The gap between CV point estimates and lower CIs is relatively consistent (0.022-0.026), indicating stable uncertainty quantification.
